children.heading: City
children.label: 1 City
children.text: The term “City” means the city of Durango, Colorado.

children.heading: County
children.label: 2 County
children.text: The term “County” means La Plata County, Colorado.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 3 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: Special Management Area
children.label: 4 Special Management Area
children.text: The term “Special Management Area” means the Hermosa Creek Special Management Area designated by subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: State
children.label: 5 State
children.text: The term “State” means the State of Colorado.

children.heading: Designation of Hermosa Creek Special Management Area
children.label: b Designation of Hermosa Creek Special Management Area

children.heading: Designation
children.label: 1 Designation
children.text: Subject to valid existing rights, certain Federal land in the San Juan National Forest comprising approximately 70,650 acres, as generally depicted on the map entitled “Proposed Hermosa Creek Special Management Area and Proposed Hermosa Creek Wilderness Area” and dated , is designated as the “Hermosa Creek Special Management Area”.November 12, 2014

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 2 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the Special Management Area is to conserve and protect for the benefit of present and future generations the watershed, geological, cultural, natural, scientific, recreational, wildlife, riparian, historical, educational, and scenic resources of the Special Management Area.

children.label: i
children.text: in a manner that conserves, protects, and manages the resources of the Special Management Area described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: I
children.text: the National Forest Management Act of 1976 ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 1600

children.label: II
children.text: this Act; and

children.label: III
children.text: any other applicable laws.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall allow only such uses of the Special Management Area as the Secretary determines would further the purposes described in paragraph (2).1So in original. Probably should be “purpose”.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Except as provided in subclause (II) and as needed for administrative purposes or to respond to an emergency, the use of motorized or mechanized vehicles in the Special Management Area shall be permitted only on roads and trails designated by the Secretary for use by those vehicles.

children.label: aa
children.text: when there exists adequate snow coverage; and

children.label: bb
children.text: subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Grazing
children.label: iii Grazing
children.text: The Secretary shall permit grazing within the Special Management Area, if established before , subject to all applicable laws (including regulations) and Executive orders.December 19, 2014

children.label: I
children.text: New permanent or temporary road construction or the renovation of existing nonsystem roads, except as allowed under the final rule entitled “Special Areas; Roadless Area Conservation; Applicability to the National Forests in Colorado” (77 Fed. Reg. 39576 ()).July 3, 2012

children.label: II
children.text: Projects undertaken for the purpose of harvesting commercial timber (other than activities relating to the harvest of merchantable products that are byproducts of activities conducted for ecological restoration or to further the purposes described in this section).

children.label: A
children.text: pages 17 through 20 of the Statewide Water Supply Initiative studies prepared by the Colorado Water Conservation Board and issued by the State in November 2004; and

children.label: B
children.text: page 27 of the Colorado Dam Site Inventory prepared by the Colorado Water Conservation Board and dated August 1996.

children.label: i
children.text: all forms of entry, appropriation, and disposal under the public land laws;

children.label: ii
children.text: location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and

children.label: iii
children.text: operation of the mineral leasing, mineral materials, and geothermal leasing laws.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: The withdrawal under subparagraph (A) shall not apply to the areas identified as parcels A and B on the map entitled “Proposed Hermosa Creek Special Management Area and Proposed Hermosa Creek Wilderness Area” and dated .November 12, 2014

children.heading: Winter skiing and related winter activities
children.label: 6 Winter skiing and related winter activities
A
children.text: a permit held by a ski area;

children.label: B
children.text: the implementation of the activities governed by a ski area permit; or

children.label: C
children.text: the authority of the Secretary to modify or expand an existing ski area permit.

children.label: i
children.text: such reasonable regulations, policies, and practices as the Secretary determines to be appropriate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: all applicable laws (including regulations); and

children.label: i
children.text: the purposes described in paragraph (2); and1

children.label: ii
children.text: this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: carry out any measures that the Secretary determines to be necessary to manage wildland fire and treat hazardous fuels, insects, and diseases in the Special Management Area; and

children.label: B
children.text: coordinate those measures with the appropriate State or local agency, as the Secretary determines to be necessary.

children.label: A
children.text: takes into account public input; and

children.label: B
children.text: provides for recreational opportunities to occur within the Special Management Area, including skiing, biking, hiking, fishing, hunting, horseback riding, snowmobiling, motorcycle riding, off-highway vehicle use, snowshoeing, and camping.

children.heading: Trail and open area snowmobile usage
children.label: 10 Trail and open area snowmobile usage
children.text: Nothing in this subsection affects the use or status of trails authorized for motorized or mechanized vehicle or open area snowmobile use on .December 19, 2014

children.label: A
children.text: decreed under the laws of the State; and

children.label: B
children.text: in existence on .December 19, 2014

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 2 Effective date
children.text: Any reference contained in the Wilderness Act ( et seq.) to the effective date of that Act shall be considered to be a reference to , for purposes of administering the wilderness area designated by section 2(a)(22) of the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 ( note; ; ; ) (as added by paragraph (1)).16 U.S.C. 113116 U.S.C. 1132107 Stat. 756114 Stat. 1955116 Stat. 10552December 19, 2014See Codification note below.2

children.heading: Fire, insects, and diseases
children.label: 3 Fire, insects, and diseases
children.text: In accordance with section 4(d)(1) of the Wilderness Act (), within the wilderness areas designated by section 2(a)(22) of the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 ( note; ; ; ) (as added by paragraph (1)), the Secretary may carry out any measure that the Secretary determines to be necessary to control fire, insects, and diseases, subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.16 U.S.C. 1133(d)(1)16 U.S.C. 1132107 Stat. 756114 Stat. 1955116 Stat. 10552

children.label: A
children.text: entry, appropriation, and disposal under the public land laws;

children.label: B
children.text: location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and

children.label: C
children.text: disposition under all laws relating to mineral leasing, geothermal leasing, or mineral materials.

children.heading: Description of land and mineral interests
children.label: 2 Description of land and mineral interests
children.text: The land and mineral interests referred to in paragraph (1) are the Federal land and mineral interests generally depicted within the areas designated as “Withdrawal Areas” on the map entitled “Perins Peak & Animas City Mountain, Horse Gulch and Lake Nighthorse Mineral Withdrawal” and dated .April 5, 2013

children.label: A
children.text: pursuant to the Act of (commonly known as the “Recreation and Public Purposes Act”) ( et seq.); orJune 14, 192643 U.S.C. 869

children.label: B
children.text: by exchange in accordance with applicable laws (including regulations).

children.heading: Conveyance of Bureau of Land Management land to County
children.label: e Conveyance of Bureau of Land Management land to County
A
children.text: paragraph (3);

children.label: B
children.text: the condition that the County shall pay all administrative and other costs associated with the conveyance; and

children.label: C
children.text: such other terms and conditions as the Secretary of the Interior determines to be necessary.

children.heading: Description of land
children.label: 2 Description of land
children.text: The land referred to in paragraph (1) consists of approximately 82 acres of land managed by the Bureau of Land Management, Tres Rios District, Colorado, as generally depicted on the map entitled “La Plata County Grandview Conveyance” and dated .May 5, 2014

children.heading: Use of conveyed land
children.label: 3 Use of conveyed land
children.text: The Federal land conveyed pursuant to this subsection may be used by the County for any public purpose, in accordance with the Act of (commonly known as the “Recreation and Public Purposes Act”) ( et seq.).June 14, 192643 U.S.C. 869

children.heading: Reversion
children.label: 4 Reversion
children.text: If the County ceases to use a parcel of the Federal land conveyed pursuant to this subsection in accordance with paragraph (1), title to the parcel shall revert to the Secretary of the Interior, at the option of the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Molas Pass Recreation Area; Wilderness Study Area release; Wilderness Study Area transfer of administrative jurisdiction
children.label: f Molas Pass Recreation Area; Wilderness Study Area release; Wilderness Study Area transfer of administrative jurisdiction

children.heading: Designation
children.label: A Designation
children.text: The approximately 461 acres of land in San Juan County, Colorado, that is generally depicted as “Molas Pass Recreation Area” on the map entitled “Molas Pass Recreation Area and Molas Pass Wilderness Study Area” and dated , is designated as the “Molas Pass Recreation Area”.November 13, 2014

children.label: i
children.text: during periods of adequate snow coverage;

children.label: ii
children.text: in accordance with the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ( et seq.) and other applicable laws (including regulations);43 U.S.C. 1701

children.label: iii
children.text: on designated trails for winter motorized travel and grooming;

children.label: iv
children.text: in designated areas for open area motorized travel; and

children.label: v
children.text: subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Other recreational opportunities
children.label: C Other recreational opportunities
children.text: In addition to the uses authorized under subparagraph (B), the Secretary may authorize other recreational uses in the Molas Pass Recreation Area.

children.heading: Transfer of administrative jurisdiction
children.label: A Transfer of administrative jurisdiction
children.text: Administrative jurisdiction over the Federal land generally depicted as “Molas Pass Wilderness Study Area” on the map entitled “Molas Pass Recreation Area and Molas Pass Wilderness Study Area”, and dated , is transferred from the Bureau of Land Management to the Forest Service.November 13, 2014

children.label: i
children.text: be known as the “Molas Pass Wilderness Study Area”; and

children.label: ii
children.text: be administered by the Secretary, so as to maintain the wilderness character and potential of the Federal land for inclusion in the National Wilderness Preservation System.

children.heading: Finding
children.label: A Finding
children.text: Congress finds that the land described in subparagraph (C) has been adequately studied for wilderness designation under section 603 of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ().43 U.S.C. 1782

children.label: i
children.text: shall not be subject to section 603(c) of the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976 ();43 U.S.C. 1782(c)

children.label: ii
children.text: shall be managed in accordance with land management plans adopted under section 202 of that Act (); and43 U.S.C. 1712

children.label: iii
children.text: shall not be subject to Secretarial Order 3310 issued on .December 22, 2010

children.heading: Description of land
children.label: C Description of land
children.text: The land referred to in subparagraphs (A) and (B) is the approximately 461 acres located in the West Needles Contiguous Wilderness Study Area of San Juan County, Colorado, that is generally depicted as “Molas Pass Recreation Area” on the map entitled “Molas Pass Recreation Area and Molas Pass Wilderness Study Area” and dated .November 13, 2014

children.heading: Fish and wildlife
children.label: 1 Fish and wildlife
children.text: Nothing in this section affects the jurisdiction or responsibility of the State with regard to fish and wildlife in the State.

children.label: i
children.text: the Special Management Area;

children.label: ii
children.text: the wilderness area designated by the amendment made by subsection (c)(1);2

children.label: iii
children.text: the withdrawal pursuant to subsection (d);

children.label: iv
children.text: the conveyance pursuant to subsection (e);

children.label: v
children.text: the recreation area designated by subsection (f)(1); and

children.label: vi
children.text: the wilderness study area designated by subsection (f)(2)(B)(i).

children.heading: Force of law
children.label: B Force of law
children.text: The maps and legal descriptions prepared under subparagraph (A) shall have the same force and effect as if included in this section, except that the Secretary concerned may correct any clerical or typographical errors in the maps and legal descriptions.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: C Public availability
children.text: The maps and legal descriptions prepared under subparagraph (A) shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the Forest Service and the Bureau of Land Management.

children.label: i
children.text: the Special Management Area;

children.label: ii
children.text: the wilderness area designated by an amendment made by subsection (c)(1); or2

children.label: iii
children.text: the wilderness study area designated by subsection (f)(2)(B)(i).

children.heading: Nonwilderness activities
children.label: B Nonwilderness activities
children.text: The fact that a nonwilderness activity or use can be seen or heard from areas within the wilderness area designated by an amendment made by subsection (c)(1) or the wilderness study area designated by subsection (f)(2)(B)(i) shall not preclude the conduct of the activity or use outside the boundary of the wilderness area or wilderness study area.2

children.label: A
children.text: any low-level overflight of military aircraft over an area designated as a wilderness area under an amendment made by this section, including military overflights that can be seen, heard, or detected within the wilderness area;2

children.label: B
children.text: flight testing or evaluation; or

children.label: i
children.text: new units of special use airspace; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any military flight training route over a wilderness area described in subparagraph (A).
